reason: Both answers correctly diagnose the core explanation (MTTR is a mean, subject to gaming/reclassification, hides distribution and recurrence) and converge on similar fixes (SLOs/error budget, incident frequency, recurrence rate, pager load, distribution not average). Content quality is comparable, but Answer A opens with a redundant sketch bullet list that essentially duplicates the prose answer below it, adding clutter without value. Answer B is more tightly organized and adds a couple of extra good insights (survivorship/classification bias for near-misses/chronic degradation never becoming incidents, and explicit sum-not-mean framing plus P90/P99). B is marginally better overall.
